opportunities.title: The $500 D&D Box: What You're Actually Paying For (Hint: Not Rules)
opportunities.why_now: Beadle & Grimm's just announced a $500 'Deadfall' boxed set, a ripe target for a dry cost-per-session breakdown.
opportunities.angle: 30-sec 'ruling' style breakdown comparing $500 swag set to a $20 Shadowdark zine — cost-per-hour-of-play math, deadpan delivery

opportunities.title: Shadowdark Doesn't Need a $500 Box
opportunities.why_now: The Deadfall set's excess is a perfect foil to spotlight Shadowdark's stripped-down design philosophy, tying beat-relevant systems together.
opportunities.angle: Split-screen comparison short: premium bloat vs. minimalist crunch, ending on a one-line design ruling

opportunities.title: One Lazy DM Habit That Kills Session Zero Bloat
opportunities.why_now: Sly Flourish's fresh 'Prepping and Running Lazy Campaigns' piece is direct fuel for a prep-focused daily short.
opportunities.angle: Veteran-GM tip format: 'Here's the one prep step you can cut this week' with a concrete example encounter

opportunities.title: What Mike Shea Actually Said You Should Steal From This Week's D&D News
opportunities.why_now: Podcast #405 with Mike Shea just dropped a 'ton of D&D news,' giving a timely hook to react with a specific mechanical takeaway.
opportunities.angle: Reaction-style short: pull one ruling/tactic mentioned in the livestream and give the blunt GM verdict on whether it's worth using

opportunities.title: Killer Clowns 101: Running Comedy Villains Without Killing the Tension
opportunities.why_now: EN World's 'Send in the Clowns' piece is a ready-made creature/NPC tactic angle for the daily beat.
opportunities.angle: Creature tactic breakdown: how to stat and run a trickster/clown NPC (fear + humor balance) in 5e/DCC without derailing the table
